creator: xistence@0x58.com
text:
(In reply to Martin Wilke from comment #6)

Unfortunately there is no progress, I am no longer at the company that was using FreeBSD with OpenStack, and haven't had a chance to spin up a VM with a cloud provider to test the latest version, but I have a feeling it too is broken...

I posted previous that 0.7.8 was broken completely on FreeBSD, I still have open bug reports on Launchpad. Unfortunately the amount of Linuxism's used within cloud-init, and the way its platform support is not properly split from the various parts of the project means it is a lot of time and effort to try and figure out why it is not working or getting patches into the project.

Here are the current open bug reports:

https://bugs.launchpad.net/cloud-init/+bug/1636345
https://bugs.launchpad.net/cloud-init/+bug/1404745
https://bugs.launchpad.net/cloud-init/+bug/1364580
https://bugs.launchpad.net/cloud-init/+bug/1412094
https://bugs.launchpad.net/cloud-init/+bug/1625802
https://bugs.launchpad.net/cloud-init/+bug/1645824

Unfortunately some of them have been open for a long time. Since the project is a Canonical project, there is almost zero interest from upstream for operating systems outside of Ubuntu/RHEL (the ones they support on their private cloud deployments).

I don't know if it is worth closing the ticket or not. Every so often I get the email reminding me about this ticket, and I go check and see if there has been any movement on the issues above, but am always left disappointed.

Bert
